description: vice president of the United States from 1801 to 1805, lived (1756–1836)

description_wiki: Alexander Hamilton (January 11, 1755 or 1757 – July 12, 1804) was an American military officer, statesman, and Founding Father who served as the first U.S. secretary of the treasury from 1789 to 1795 under the presidency of George Washington.

surface_forms: New College on the Franklin campus of the university

description: consolidated city-county and county seat of Clarke County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Athens is a consolidated city-county in the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: city in Lumpkin County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Dahlonega ( də-LON-ig-ə) is the county seat of Lumpkin County, Georgia, United States.

description: city in Jackson County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Jefferson is a city and the county seat of Jackson County, Georgia, United States.

description: city in Gwinnett County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Sugar Hill is a city in northern Gwinnett County in the U.S. state of Georgia, included within the Metro Atlanta area.

description: state capitol building of the U.S. state of Georgia
description_wiki: The Georgia State Capitol is an architecturally and historically significant building in Atlanta, Georgia, United States.
The Martin Institute was a school in Jefferson, Georgia, United States, from 1818 to 1942.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: city in Oconee County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Watkinsville is the largest city and county seat of Oconee County, Georgia, United States.
